title: Map Gallery
text:

      Grizzly Bear Habitat & Least Cost Path
      Raster analysis and least cost path modeling of Grizzly Bear movement in western Alberta.
    
  

  
    
    
      Old-Growth Forest Coverage Deficit
      Spatial analysis of old-growth shortfall from high-threshold CEF targets on Vancouver Island.
    
  

  
    
    
      Salmon Spawning Habitat Network
      Hydrologic network analysis and salmon spawning habitat identification on Vancouver Island.
    
  

  
    
    
      Identifying Illegal Urban Solid Waste Dumps
      Supervised classification and spectral signature analysis to map illegal waste sites in Cd. Obregon, Sonora.

title: Interactive Projects
text:
CAP Potential is an interactive 3D visualization exploring how vineyard terrain influences the spatial accumulation of cold air. During calm, stable nighttime conditions, dense cooled air moves downslope and settles in low-lying areas, a process particularly relevant to viticulture where frost during critical growth stages can cause significant crop damage.
The analysis uses a high-resolution digital terrain model (DTM) derived from UAV photogrammetry. A Topographic Position Index (TPI) was applied to identify terrain depressions, while flow accumulation was used to represent cold-air drainage pathways. Combining both metrics allowed the vineyard to be classified by its relative susceptibility to cold-air
The 3D view makes subtle elevation differences and drainage patterns far more interpretable than traditional 2D maps, supporting site-specific frost risk awareness and vineyard management planning.

  
  
    Cold Air Pooling Potential
    
      
      High
    
    
      
      Moderate
    
    
      
      Low
    
    
      
      No CAP
    
  




LiDAR (Light Detection and Ranging) captures the three-dimensional structure of forest canopies with exceptional detail, making it a powerful tool for individual tree detection and forest inventory. This analysis uses a normalized LiDAR point cloud collected at the Malcolm Knapp Research Forest (MKRF) in Maple Ridge, British Columbia, a mixed conifer research forest managed by the University of British Columbia.
Individual trees were segmented using the Li et al. (2012) point cloud-based algorithm, which works from the treetop downward, progressively assigning points to individual trees based on their relative spacing and height. Each detected tree is assigned a unique ID and colored accordingly, allowing direct visualization of crown boundaries, tree density, and stand structure.
The interactive viewer below allows you to rotate, zoom, and explore the segmented point cloud in 3D.




BloomWatch is an interactive web application developed as part of a research project analyzing chlorophyll concentration in coastal water bodies in southern Sonora, Mexico, using Sentinel-2 satellite imagery processed through Google Earth Engine.
The app allows users to visually compare the Normalized Difference Chlorophyll Index (NDCI) between any two years, side by side, with synchronized maps and a monthly time selector. The default comparison is set to January, historically the peak of the wheat production season in the region, making it particularly relevant for studying the relationship between agricultural activity and aquatic chlorophyll levels.
The 2024-2025 winter served as a key control period for this study. Due to severe water scarcity, the local irrigation district denied 98% of agricultural water permits, significantly reducing irrigation runoff as a nutrient input. This created a rare natural experiment to isolate and observe chlorophyll behavior under minimal agricultural influence, providing valuable insight into algae bloom dynamics in the region.

This Google Earth Engine app allows you to explore and visualize NDCI patterns across years and months around the world.

title: Resume
text:
Master of Geomatics for Environmental Management
University of British Columbia
2025 - Present

B.Eng. Environmental Sciences
Sonora Institute of Technology
2018 - 2022


Graduate Research Assistant
University of British Columbia, Faculty of Arts
Collaborative Digital Heritage Studio
Vancouver, Canada
2025 - Present

Developed interactive web mapping experiences (ArcGIS Experience Builder, StoryMaps) for Indigenous cultural preservation and storytelling.



Translated research into accessible, public-facing content through cross-team collaboration.

Project Manager
642 Studio, Digital Marketing & SAAS
Sonora, Mexico
2024-2025

Coordinated concurrent client projects, managing scope, timelines, deliverables, and documentation.



Liaised across clients, vendors, and internal teams, providing updates and supporting QA to keep delivery on track.

Undergraduate Research Assistant
Sonora Institute of Technology
Water and Environmental Sciences Department
Sonora, Mexico
2021-2022

Supported climate impacts research via data collection, dataset management, and reproducible statistical analysis in R.



Analyzed 400+ wheat leaf imprint samples and contributed to literature review and technical writing.

Environmental Intern Lead
Rubisco, Environmental Solutions Consulting
Sonora, Mexico
2020-2021

Directed a 4-person project delivering GIS and remote sensing analysis of illegal waste disposal impacts to inform sustainability planning.



Produced georeferenced high-tension tower locations, interpreted UAV-based observations to support infrastructure condition reporting and risk documentation.
